hero.title: Fresh herbs,
hero.description: Blackpaw Cottage is a small home garden tucked behind a cedar fence. When the season blesses us with more basil, rosemary, or mint than we can use — we share. No strings, no sign-ups, just good growing.

about.paragraph1: In 2024, my life was turned upside down. But what started out as a story of heartbreak turned into one of the most transformative years of my life. Not only did I learn what I'm truly capable of, but it brought me to Cooper; my adorable black Cocker Spaniel (you may have heard him 🙈), who ultimately led us here to Blackpaw Cottage - the first place in a really long time where I feel truly...home.
about.paragraph2:
I'm sure by now you can guess what (or rather, who) inspired the name Blackpaw Cottage - but what most don't know is that I'm the kind of dog mum who buys a house so her baby can have a big backyard to run around in. What I didn't account for was that backyards don't magically look after themselves - especially not one lovingly created by someone who poured her heart and soul into it; from herbs and fruit trees to beautiful native flowers.

Over the past 12 months, I've been fumbling my way through figuring out how to keep it all alive - and through absolute luck (and a little naive optimism), my garden is not just surviving, it's somehow... thriving.
about.paragraph3:
I'm now growing a lot more than I can use and because my love language is acts of service, I  shared a post in our Facebook group asking if anyone in our community would like some of my excess herbs. And to my delight - so many of you said yes!

I love going the extra mile, especially if I think it'll make someone smile, so instead of just handing you a bunch of herbs, I had some cute gift tags designed so that I could leave little handwritten notes from Cooper and I.

One of my closest friends has been watching all of this unfold and I mentioned recently how fun it would be to have a website my neighbours could "order from" instead of trying to keep track of all the  comments and DMs each time I post.

He immediately took that idea and brought this amazing website to life for me!

herbs.name: Sweet Basil
herbs.description: Large, fragrant leaves perfect for caprese, pesto, or tossing fresh into pasta. The Italian classic.
herbs.tip: Pick from the top to encourage bushy growth.

herbs.name: Rosemary
herbs.description: Woody stems full of piney aroma. Great for roasted meats, bread, or in a vase on the windowsill.
herbs.tip: Cut 4-6 inch sprigs, just above a leaf node.

herbs.name: Spearmint
herbs.description: Cool, clean mint for tea, mojitos, tabbouleh, or just a sprig in a glass of water.
herbs.tip: Best harvested in the morning before it gets hot.

herbs.name: Flat-Leaf Parsley
herbs.description: Bright, grassy flavor — far better fresh than dried. Goes with almost everything savory.
herbs.tip: Cut outer stems at the base to keep it producing.

herbs.name: Lemon Thyme
herbs.description: Tiny leaves with a citrusy twist on classic thyme. Wonderful with fish, poultry, or desserts.
herbs.tip: The flavor is most intense before it flowers.

herbs.name: Chives
herbs.description: Mild, oniony flavor for topping eggs, potatoes, and soups. The purple flowers are edible too.
herbs.tip: Will be ready to share in 2-3 weeks.

herbs.name: Oregano
herbs.description: The backbone of Italian and Greek cooking. We harvested heavily this year — resting the plant.

herbs.name: Tarragon
herbs.description: Anise-like and distinctly French. Elevates chicken, eggs, and cream sauces to something special.
herbs.tip: A little goes a long way — use sparingly.
